This is a historical architectural blueprint on aged, cream-colored paper showing detailed technical drawings of a building. The main composition features a longitudinal section view at the bottom center, displaying a large Victorian-style structure with multiple peaked gables, a prominent tall spire or tower on the left side, and various interior levels with detailed cross-hatching indicating different materials and construction methods. The building includes ornamental railings, columns, multiple stories, and various architectural embellishments typical of 19th-century design.

# Architectural Floor Plan - First Floor

This is a detailed architectural floor plan drawn on aged, cream-colored paper with visible stains and wear marks. At the top of the document is a measurement scale bar with colored sections (navy, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, white, and dark gray). The plan shows the first floor layout of a multi-story building designed by "E. M. Dougall & Son, Archts." located at "330 Pine St, S.F." (San Francisco), as noted in the bottom right corner.

The layout features a symmetrical, formal design with multiple rooms labeled and dimensioned. Central to the plan is a large "Hall" space, with flanking "Children's Dining Room" and "Old Ladies' Dining Room" on either side of an upper section. Below are additional spaces including "Bed Rooms," a "Matron's Room," "Sitting Rooms," "Kitchens," and various other service areas. The ground floor (lower section) contains multiple "Bed Rooms," "Halls," a "Vestibule," and "Sitting Rooms" arranged along corridors.

The plan includes handwritten notations indicating room dimensions, doorways marked with "d," windows with "w," and various architectural details. A notation at the bottom states "Scale: Eight Feet To One Inch." The document shows the characteristic style of early-to-mid 20th-century institutional or residential architecture, likely for a charitable or medical facility.
